ucs.title: [LLM] APT42 (Charming Kitten / Mint Sandstorm) credential-harvest click followed by token-replay sign-in to M365
ucs.description: APT42's signature initial-access chain: highly-tailored spearphishing-link email pointing to a look-alike Microsoft/Google sign-in page hosted on free PaaS (Google Sites, Glitch, workers.dev, firebaseapp), credential + session-cookie theft, then session-token replay against Exchange Online or Graph from a previously-unseen geography within hours. This UC time-correlates the Safe Links click to the subsequent suspicious sign-in by the same UPN to surface the exact APT42 kill-chain documented by Mandiant (M-Trends 2023/2024) and Microsoft (Mint Sandstorm).
ucs.splunk: | tstats `summariesonly` min(_time) as click_time values(Web.url) as click_url from datamodel=Web.Web where Web.action=allowed AND (Web.url="*sites.google.com*" OR Web.url="*.glitch.me*" OR Web.url="*.web.app*" OR Web.url="*.workers.dev*" OR Web.url="*.pages.dev*" OR Web.url="*firebaseapp.com*" OR Web.url="*.repl.co*" OR Web.url="*.onrender.com*" OR Web.url="*.herokuapp.com*") AND (Web.url="*login*" OR Web.url="*signin*" OR Web.url="*office*" OR Web.url="*microsoft*" OR Web.url="*outlook*" OR Web.url="*o365*") by Web.user Web.src | rename Web.user as user Web.src as src_click | append [| tstats `summariesonly` min(_time) as logon_time values(Authentication.src) as logon_src values(Authentication.app) as app values(Authentication.signature) as sig from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication where Authentication.action=success AND Authentication.app IN ("office365","exchange","graph","AzureAD") by Authentication.user Authentication.src_country | rename Authentication.user as user Authentication.src_country as logon_country] | stats min(click_time) as click_time max(logon_time) as logon_time values(click_url) as click_url values(logon_country) as logon_country values(logon_src) as logon_src by user | where logon_time > click_time AND (logon_time - click_time) < 14400 | lookup user_baseline_country user OUTPUT known_countries | where NOT match(logon_country, known_countries) | eval delay_min=round((logon_time - click_time)/60,1) | table click_time logon_time delay_min user click_url logon_country logon_src
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let WindowMinutes = 240;
// 1. Click on a credential-harvester hosted on free PaaS — APT42's documented infra preference
let SuspectClicks = UrlClickEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where ActionType in ("ClickAllowed","ClickedThrough")
    | where Url has_any ("sites.google.com",".glitch.me",".web.app",".workers.dev",
                         ".pages.dev","firebaseapp.com",".repl.co",".onrender.com",
                         ".herokuapp.com",".000webhostapp.com","r2.dev")
    | where Url has_any ("login","signin","office","microsoft","outlook","o365","auth","verify")
    | project ClickTime = Timestamp, AccountUpn, Url, NetworkMessageId;
// 2. Per-user 30d country baseline
let UserBaselineCountry = AADSignInEventsBeta
    | where Timestamp between (ago(30d) .. ago(LookbackDays))
    | where ErrorCode == 0
    | summarize KnownCountries = make_set(Country), KnownASN = make_set(IPAddress) by AccountUpn;
// 3. Successful sign-in to Exchange / Graph / SPO within 4h of click, from new country
AADSignInEventsBeta
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where ErrorCode == 0                                  // 0 = success
| where ApplicationId in ("00000002-0000-0ff1-ce00-000000000000",   // EXO
                           "00000003-0000-0000-c000-000000000000",   // Graph
                           "00000003-0000-0ff1-ce00-000000000000")   // SPO
| join kind=inner SuspectClicks on $left.AccountUpn == $right.AccountUpn
| where Timestamp between (ClickTime .. ClickTime + WindowMinutes * 1m)
| join kind=leftouter UserBaselineCountry on AccountUpn
| extend IsNewCountry = iff(isnotempty(Country) and Country !in (KnownCountries), true, false)
| where IsNewCountry == true or IsAnonymousProxy == true or RiskLevelDuringSignIn in ("medium","high")
| project ClickTime, SignInTime = Timestamp, DelayMin = datetime_diff('minute', Timestamp, ClickTime),
          AccountUpn, Url, IPAddress, Country, City, Application,
          IsAnonymousProxy, RiskLevelDuringSignIn, ClientAppUsed
| order by SignInTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Sentinel approximation — UrlClickEvents has no clean Sentinel equivalent.
// Detect the post-harvest token replay: a successful EXO/Graph sign-in from a
// country never seen before on that UPN, soon after an inbound delivered email
// containing a credential-harvester URL on free PaaS (APT42 infra pattern).
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let WindowMinutes = 240;
let HarvesterDomains = dynamic(["sites.google.com","glitch.me","web.app","workers.dev",
                                "pages.dev","firebaseapp.com","repl.co","onrender.com",
                                "herokuapp.com","000webhostapp.com","r2.dev"]);
let SuspectMail = OfficeActivity
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where OfficeWorkload == "Exchange" and Operation has_any ("MailItemsAccessed","MessageReceived")
    | extend Body = tostring(Parameters)
    | where Body has_any (HarvesterDomains)
    | where Body has_any ("login","signin","office","microsoft","outlook","verify")
    | project MailTime = TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName = MailboxOwnerUPN;
let KnownCountry = SigninLogs
    | where TimeGenerated between (ago(30d) .. ago(LookbackDays))
    | where ResultType == 0
    | extend Country = tostring(parse_json(LocationDetails).countryOrRegion)
    | summarize KnownCountries = make_set(Country) by UserPrincipalName;
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where ResultType == 0
| where AppDisplayName has_any ("Office 365 Exchange","Microsoft Graph","Office 365 SharePoint","OfficeHome","Outlook")
| extend Country = tostring(parse_json(LocationDetails).countryOrRegion)
| join kind=inner SuspectMail on UserPrincipalName
| where TimeGenerated between (MailTime .. MailTime + WindowMinutes * 1m)
| join kind=leftouter KnownCountry on UserPrincipalName
| extend IsNewCountry = iff(isnotempty(Country) and Country !in (KnownCountries), true, false)
| where IsNewCountry == true or RiskLevelDuringSignIn in ("medium","high")
| project MailTime, SignInTime = TimeGenerated,
          DelayMin = datetime_diff('minute', TimeGenerated, MailTime),
          UserPrincipalName, IPAddress, Country, AppDisplayName,
          ClientAppUsed, RiskLevelDuringSignIn, ConditionalAccessStatus
| order by SignInTime desc
ucs.rationale: APT42 (Charming Kitten / Mint Sandstorm / TA453 / Yellow Garuda) is documented by Mandiant and Microsoft as relying almost exclusively on long-tail, persona-driven spearphishing (academic/journalist personas) that leads victims to credential-harvester pages hosted on legitimate free PaaS — Google Sites, Glitch, workers.dev, firebaseapp — to bypass URL reputation. The campaign goal is mailbox / Gra

ucs.title: [LLM] APT42 NICECURL / TAMECAT VBS-to-PowerShell loader spawning from Office or wscript
ucs.description: APT42 routinely drops VBS (NICECURL) or PowerShell (TAMECAT/POWERPOST) backdoors via spearphishing attachments — the loader chain runs wscript.exe or an Office app spawning powershell.exe with downloader cradles, then resolves second-stage staging from a benign-looking web service (Google Drive, Dropbox, GitHub raw, Telegram bot API) per APT42's T1102 web-service C2 tradecraft. This UC catches the parent→child→cradle pattern that distinguishes the loader from generic macro-malware.
ucs.splunk: | tstats `summariesonly` count min(_time) as first_seen values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.process_hash) as sha256 values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") AND Processes.parent_process_name IN ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe","winword.exe","excel.exe","outlook.exe","mshta.exe","explorer.exe") AND (Processes.process="*DownloadString*" OR Processes.process="*DownloadFile*" OR Processes.process="*Invoke-WebRequest*" OR Processes.process="*Invoke-Expression*" OR Processes.process="* IEX *" OR Processes.process="*FromBase64String*" OR Processes.process="*-EncodedCommand*" OR Processes.process="* -enc *" OR Processes.process="*Net.WebClient*" OR Processes.process="*WebRequest*") AND NOT Processes.user IN ("*$","SYSTEM","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name | rename Processes.* as * | join type=left dest [| tstats `summariesonly` values(All_Traffic.dest) as c2_dest values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as c2_port from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.app IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") AND (All_Traffic.dest="*googleusercontent.com" OR All_Traffic.dest="*drive.google.com" OR All_Traffic.dest="*dropbox*" OR All_Traffic.dest="*githubusercontent.com" OR All_Traffic.dest="*api.telegram.org" OR All_Traffic.dest="*onedrive.live.com" OR All_Traffic.dest="*pastebin*") by All_Traffic.src | rename All_Traffic.src as dest] | where isnotnull(c2_dest) | table first_seen dest user parent process_name cmdline c2_dest c2_port sha256
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let ScriptHosts = dynamic(["wscript.exe","cscript.exe","winword.exe","excel.exe",
                          "powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","mshta.exe","explorer.exe"]);
let DownloaderTokens = dynamic(["DownloadString","DownloadFile","Invoke-WebRequest",
                                "Invoke-Expression"," IEX "," iex(","FromBase64String",
                                "-EncodedCommand"," -enc "," -ec ","Net.WebClient",
                                "WebRequest","Start-BitsTransfer"]);
let APT42WebSvc = dynamic(["googleusercontent.com","drive.google.com",
                           "dropboxusercontent.com","dropbox.com",
                           "githubusercontent.com","api.telegram.org",
                           "onedrive.live.com","sharepoint.com",
                           "pastebin.com","hastebin.com","sites.google.com"]);
// Stage 1 — Office/wscript spawning PowerShell with a downloader cradle
let LoaderSpawn = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (ScriptHosts)
    | where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any (DownloaderTokens)
    | project SpawnTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine, SHA256, ProcessId;
// Stage 2 — same PowerShell PID resolves a second-stage on a legit web service (APT42 T1102)
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
| where RemoteIPType == "Public"
| where RemoteUrl has_any (APT42WebSvc)
   or RemoteUrl matches regex @"(?i)\.(workers\.dev|web\.app|pages\.dev|glitch\.me|firebaseapp\.com)$"
| join kind=inner LoaderSpawn on DeviceId, $left.InitiatingProcessId == $right.ProcessId
| where Timestamp between (SpawnTime .. SpawnTime + 5m)
| project SpawnTime, NetTime = Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ParentImage, ParentCmd, ChildCmd, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, SHA256
| order by SpawnTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let ScriptHosts = dynamic(["wscript.exe","cscript.exe","winword.exe","excel.exe",
                          "powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","mshta.exe","explorer.exe"]);
let DownloaderTokens = dynamic(["DownloadString","DownloadFile","Invoke-WebRequest",
                                "Invoke-Expression"," IEX ","FromBase64String",
                                "-EncodedCommand"," -enc "," -ec ","Net.WebClient",
                                "WebRequest","Start-BitsTransfer"]);
let APT42WebSvc = dynamic(["googleusercontent.com","drive.google.com",
                           "dropboxusercontent.com","dropbox.com",
                           "githubusercontent.com","api.telegram.org",
                           "onedrive.live.com","sharepoint.com",
                           "pastebin.com","hastebin.com","sites.google.com"]);
let LoaderSpawn = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | where ParentProcessName has_any (ScriptHosts)
    | where NewProcessName endswith "powershell.exe" or NewProcessName endswith "pwsh.exe"
    | where CommandLine has_any (DownloaderTokens)
    | project SpawnTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
              ParentProcessName, CommandLine, NewProcessId;
LoaderSpawn
| join kind=inner (
    ImNetworkSession
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where ActingProcessName has_any ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
    | where DstHostname has_any (APT42WebSvc)
       or DstHostname matches regex @"(?i)\.(workers\.dev|web\.app|pages\.dev|glitch\.me|firebaseapp\.com)$"
    | project NetTime = TimeGenerated, Computer = DvcHostname, DstHostname, DstIpAddr
  ) on Computer
| where NetTime between (SpawnTime .. SpawnTime + 5m)
| project SpawnTime, NetTime, Computer, Account,
          ParentProcessName, CommandLine, DstHostname, DstIpAddr
| order by SpawnTime desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT42 NICECURL/TAMECAT Loader - Script Host Spawns PowerShell Downloader Cradle
id: a4274218-42ce-4f01-9f82-6e1cbb9a4242
status: experimental
description: Detects APT42 (Charming Kitten / Mint Sandstorm) loader chain where wscript/cscript or an Office application spawns powershell.exe with a downloader cradle (DownloadString, IEX, EncodedCommand, FromBase64String) used by the NICECURL VBS or TAMECAT/POWERPOST PowerShell backdoors.
references:
    - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G1044/
    - https://cloud.google.com/blog/topics/threat-intelligence/apt42-charms-cons-compromises
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/04
tags:
    - attack.execution
    - attack.t1059.001
    - attack.t1059.005
    - attack.t1102
    - attack.g1044
logsource:
    category: process_creation
    product: windows
detection:
    selection_parent:
        ParentImage|endswith:
            - '\wscript.exe'
            - '\cscript.exe'
            - '\winword.exe'
            - '\excel.exe'
            - '\powerpnt.exe'
            - '\outlook.exe'
            - '\mshta.exe'
    selection_child:
        Image|endswith:
            - '\powershell.exe'
            - '\pwsh.exe'
    selection_cradle:
        CommandLine|contains:
            - 'DownloadString'
            - 'DownloadFile'
            - 'Invoke-WebRequest'
            - 'Invoke-Expression'
            - ' IEX '
            - 'FromBase64String'
            - '-EncodedCommand'
            - ' -enc '
            - ' -ec '
            - 'Net.WebClient'
            - 'Start-BitsTransfer'
    filter_machine_account:
        User|endswith: '$'
    condition: selection_parent and selection_child and selection_cradle and not filter_machine_account
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate IT automation that wraps PowerShell downloads behind wscript/cscript launchers (rare, baseline before alerting)
    - Software deployment tools using Office templates that invoke PowerShell
level: high
ucs.rationale: APT42's documented backdoor families — NICECURL (VBS) and TAMECAT/POWERPOST (PowerShell) — are dropped by spearphishing attachments that detonate via wscript/cscript or Office, then chain into PowerShell with downloader cradles to fetch second-stage payloads from legitimate web services (Google Drive, Dropbox, GitHub, Telegram Bot API) per Mandiant's APT42 reporting and Microsoft's Mint Sandstorm

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `ActingProcessName` is not a column of ImNetworkSession, SecurityEvent; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.
